title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program – Study/Research Award (Algeria)
description: A U.S. Department of State-funded grant for U.S. citizens to conduct independent research or graduate-level study in Algeria for an academic year, in fields including humanities, social sciences, science, technology and engineering. Grantees must secure their own affiliation with an Algerian university or research institution. Funded by Fulbright/IIE with support from the U.S. Embassy in Algiers.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program (U.S. Department of State / Institute of International Education)
visaNote: Grantees must obtain an Algerian long-stay/research visa; the Fulbright Commission and U.S. Embassy in Algiers assist with arrangements.

title: Critical Language Scholarship – Arabic Program (Meknes, Morocco)
description: A fully funded U.S. Department of State summer program that sends U.S. undergraduate and graduate students to Meknes, Morocco for 8-10 weeks of intensive Arabic language and cultural immersion instruction at the Arab American Language Institute. Approximately 315 students nationwide were selected for the 2026 cohort across all CLS languages and sites. Implemented by American Councils for International Education.
org: Critical Language Scholarship Program (U.S. Department of State / American Councils for International Education)
visaNote: U.S. citizens generally enter Morocco visa-free for stays under 90 days; CLS coordinates any required paperwork with the host institute and homestay families.

title: AIMS Short-Term Research Grant (Tunisia)
description: The American Institute for Maghrib Studies awards research grants of up to $6,500 (1-3 months) or $15,500 (longer projects) to U.S. scholars and graduate students conducting fieldwork in Tunisia, inclusive of airfare to/from the U.S. Grantees are affiliated with AIMS's Tunis-based overseas research center (CEMAT) and must present their research there and submit a completion report.
visaNote: Grantees must arrange their own Tunisian entry/research visa; AIMS's Tunis center (CEMAT) assists with local logistics.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program – Study/Research Award (Senegal)
description: A U.S. Department of State-funded grant for U.S. citizens to pursue independent research or graduate study in Senegal for an academic year, working with a host institution in Dakar or elsewhere in the country. French proficiency is required and study of Wolof is recommended for fieldwork. Subject to approval by the U.S. Embassy in Dakar.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program (U.S. Department of State / Institute of International Education)
visaNote: Grantees must secure a Senegalese long-stay visa/residence permit; French proficiency is necessary for daily life and research.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program – Study/Research Award (Cote d'Ivoire)
description: A U.S. Department of State-funded grant for U.S. citizens to conduct independent research or graduate study in Cote d'Ivoire for an academic year. Fluent French is required to take or audit university courses or conduct research, and applicants must arrange their own affiliation with a host institution in Abidjan or elsewhere. Subject to approval by the U.S. Embassy in Abidjan.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program (U.S. Department of State / Institute of International Education)
visaNote: Grantees must obtain an Ivorian long-stay visa; fluent French is required for university-level research and daily life.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program – Study/Research Award (Ethiopia)
description: A U.S. Department of State-funded grant for U.S. citizens to conduct independent research or graduate study in Ethiopia for an academic year. Ph.D. candidates conducting dissertation research are preferred, though Master's and Bachelor's-level candidates with relevant professional experience are also considered. Affiliation with a public university is strongly recommended, since NGO-affiliated grantees have faced difficulty obtaining residency permits.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program (U.S. Department of State / Institute of International Education)
visaNote: Grantees must obtain an Ethiopian research/residence permit; affiliation with a public university (rather than an NGO) is strongly recommended.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program – Study/Research Award (Tanzania)
description: A U.S. Department of State-funded grant for U.S. citizens to conduct independent research or graduate study in Tanzania for an academic year, requiring affiliation with an accredited Tanzanian academic or research institution. Swahili proficiency is not required for selection but is recommended for deeper engagement with the host community. Research conducted in Zanzibar requires separate clearance from the Office of the Chief Minister.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program (U.S. Department of State / Institute of International Education)
visaNote: Grantees must obtain a Tanzanian research permit/visa; research in Zanzibar requires additional clearance from the Office of the Chief Minister.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program – Study/Research Award (Uganda)
description: A U.S. Department of State-funded grant for U.S. citizens to conduct independent research or graduate study in Uganda for an academic year. The program particularly encourages projects in area/culture studies, natural resources and conservation, the visual and performing arts, and the biological/biomedical sciences, or that support health, education and democratic-institution priorities of the U.S. Mission. Subject to approval by the U.S. Embassy in Kampala.
org: Fulbright U.S. Student Program (U.S. Department of State / Institute of International Education)
visaNote: Grantees must obtain a Ugandan entry/research visa; the U.S. Embassy in Kampala facilitates arrangements.
